Title: [CRIT-1] Delegate Private Key Transmitted in Every HTTP Request
Description: The MemWal SDK class transmits the raw Ed25519 delegate private key in the x-delegate-key HTTP header on every authenticated request. The key propagates through the Rust server AuthInfo struct to the sidecar in SEAL decrypt request bodies. The MemWalManual class authenticates without this header, confirming transmission is architecturally unnecessary. AuthInfo also derives Debug (types.rs:317), creating latent risk of key leakage to logs.
Remediation: Remove x-delegate-key header from MemWal.signedRequest(). Push SEAL decryption to client following MemWalManual pattern. Implement manual Debug for AuthInfo that redacts delegate_key field.

Title: [HIGH-1] Sidecar Has Zero Authentication and Binds to All Interfaces
Description: Express sidecar has no authentication on any endpoint and binds to 0.0.0.0 by default. All endpoints (/seal/encrypt, /seal/decrypt, /walrus/upload, /sponsor, etc.) reachable from any host. No shared secret, IP allowlist, or mTLS. Wildcard CORS applied to all responses.
Remediation: Bind to 127.0.0.1 explicitly. Add shared secret middleware (X-Sidecar-Secret header). Remove CORS middleware entirely.

Title: [HIGH-2] Rate Limiter Fails Open on Redis Unavailability
Description: All three rate limit window checks catch Redis errors and unconditionally allow requests through. record_in_window also swallows recording failures silently. Docker Compose exposes Redis on 0.0.0.0:6379 with no auth, enabling deliberate crash to disable rate limiting.
Remediation: Return 429 when Redis unreachable. Implement in-memory token-bucket fallback. Bind Redis to 127.0.0.1 in Docker Compose.

Title: [HIGH-3] Analyze Endpoint Cost Amplification via Unbounded Fact Extraction
Description: /api/analyze passes user text to LLM with no cap on extracted facts. All facts processed concurrently via join_all with no concurrency bound. Each fact triggers embedding, SEAL encryption, Walrus upload, and DB insert. Rate limit cost is constant 10 regardless of fact count.
Remediation: Add facts.truncate(20) after parsing. Replace join_all with buffer_unordered(5). Adjust rate limit weight post-extraction.

Title: [HIGH-4] Unauthenticated Sponsor Proxy Endpoints
Description: POST /sponsor and /sponsor/execute on both Rust server and sidecar are public with no auth, no rate limiting, no body-size restrictions. Accept arbitrary transactionBlockKindBytes and sender values, proxied directly to Enoki API.
Remediation: Move behind Ed25519 auth middleware. Add rate limiting. Add body-size limit of 16KB.

Title: [HIGH-5] SEAL Key Server Verification Disabled in All Client Instances
Description: Every SealClient instance constructed with verifyKeyServers: false. Disables cryptographic verification of SEAL key server identity. Flag set in four separate files across sidecar and SDK. With threshold=1, single compromised server yields all key material.
Remediation: Set verifyKeyServers: true in all four files. One-line change per file.

Title: [HIGH-6] Server Wallet Private Keys Transmitted Per-Request to Sidecar
Description: On every Walrus upload, Rust server sends raw private key string in HTTP body to sidecar /walrus/upload. Key traverses plaintext HTTP (localhost but see HIGH-1). Key held in Express req.body accessible to heap dumps and error-logging middleware.
Remediation: Load SERVER_SUI_PRIVATE_KEYS at sidecar startup from env vars. Pass key index in request body instead of raw key.

Title: [HIGH-7] Delegate Private Keys Received in Sidecar Decrypt Request Bodies
Description: /seal/decrypt and /seal/decrypt-batch receive user delegate private key in JSON body. Key forwarded from AuthInfo struct. Key held in SessionKey object for 30-min TTL, accessible via heap dumps.
Remediation: Push SEAL decryption to client. Interim: reduce ttlMin from 30 to 2-5 minutes.

Title: [HIGH-8] Private Key Stored in localStorage in Frontend Apps
Description: Ed25519 delegate private key persisted to localStorage in plaintext by dashboard and chatbot apps. localStorage is unencrypted, has no expiry, accessible to any JS on same origin. Chatbot also sends stored key in POST bodies.
Remediation: Use sessionStorage as minimum. Correct solution: non-extractable CryptoKey via Web Crypto API.

Title: [HIGH-9] File Upload Path Traversal via Unsanitized Filename
Description: Filename extracted from multipart FormData and passed verbatim to Vercel Blob put(). No sanitization of path traversal sequences. No per-user namespace prefix — shared flat key space.
Remediation: Sanitize filenames. Prefix uploads with user-scoped non-guessable identifier.

Title: [HIGH-10] Missing Authorization Checks on Destructive Server Actions (IDOR)
Description: deleteTrailingMessages and updateChatVisibility Server Actions perform mutations without verifying requesting user owns affected resources. Any authenticated user can delete others' messages or change chat visibility.
Remediation: Verify resource ownership via session.user.id before performing mutations.

Title: [HIGH-11] User Enumeration via Distinct Registration Response
Description: Registration returns distinct status: user_exists when email already registered. Enables account enumeration for credential stuffing and phishing.
Remediation: Return generic failure status for both user-exists and general error cases.

Title: [HIGH-12] Open Redirect via Unvalidated redirectUrl Parameter
Description: Guest sign-in route accepts redirectUrl query param passed directly to signIn() with no validation. Attacker can craft URLs redirecting to malicious sites after auth.
Remediation: Validate redirectUrl: reject non-relative paths or different origins.

Title: [HIGH-13] No Body Size Limit on Unauthenticated Public Endpoints
Description: Public routes bypass auth middleware 1MB limit. Sidecar applies 50MB globally. /seal/decrypt-batch has no array size limit, enabling event loop blocking via thousands of items.
Remediation: Add DefaultBodyLimit::max(16384) to public routes. Reduce sidecar to 10MB. Cap decrypt-batch items at 50.

Title: [MED-1] No Replay Protection on Authenticated Requests
Description: Signed requests protected only by ±300s timestamp window with no nonce tracking. Captured requests replayable unlimited times within window.
Remediation: Add crypto.randomUUID() nonce to signed message. Track seen nonces in Redis with matching TTL.

Title: [MED-2] Deactivated Accounts Authenticate Successfully
Description: verify_delegate_key_onchain checks key existence but never reads active field. Deactivated accounts pass auth and can invoke non-SEAL endpoints, consuming storage quota.
Remediation: Read and assert account.active == true inside verify_delegate_key_onchain.

Title: [MED-3] Unbounded Concurrent Blob Downloads in /api/recall
Description: limit parameter passed directly to SQL LIMIT with no upper bound. Extreme limit spawns equal number of concurrent Walrus download and SEAL decrypt tasks via join_all.
Remediation: Cap body.limit at maximum value (e.g. 100) before database query.

Title: [MED-4] LLM Prompt Injection in /api/analyze
Description: User text injected without sanitization into LLM user message alongside system prompt. Crafted payload can override instructions, produce arbitrary facts, poison memory store.
Remediation: Cap accepted extracted facts at 20. Validate response structure before processing.

Title: [MED-5] Unbounded Fact Count Amplifies Cost in /api/analyze
Description: Extracted facts iterated without ceiling, dispatched concurrently via join_all. Each triggers embedding, SEAL encryption, Walrus upload, DB insert. Rate limit cost constant regardless of fact count.
Remediation: Apply facts.truncate(MAX_FACTS) after parsing. Replace join_all with buffer_unordered(N).

Title: [MED-6] Unbounded Concurrent Downloads in /api/restore
Description: Blob downloads dispatched with join_all without concurrency limit. SEAL decryption uses buffer_unordered(3) but downloads are unbounded. Large limit causes simultaneous outbound HTTP connections.
Remediation: Replace join_all with buffer_unordered(10) for download phase.

Title: [MED-7] No Body Size Limit on Public Sponsor Endpoints
Description: Unauthenticated /sponsor and /sponsor/execute use raw Bytes extractor bypassing 1MB auth limit. Axum default 2MB applies but repeated large payloads create memory pressure.
Remediation: Apply DefaultBodyLimit::max(16384) to public route group.

Title: [MED-8] Internal Error Messages Returned to Clients
Description: Both Rust server and sidecar return raw internal error strings including DB connection details, sidecar URL, API status codes, and LLM error responses.
Remediation: Log detailed errors server-side with correlation ID. Return generic message to clients.

Title: [MED-9] Wildcard CORS on Sidecar
Description: Sidecar sets Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * on every response. Combined with no auth and 0.0.0.0 binding, any browser context can make unrestricted cross-origin requests.
Remediation: Remove CORS headers from sidecar entirely.

Title: [MED-10] SEAL Threshold Hardcoded to 1
Description: All SEAL encrypt/decrypt operations use threshold: 1. Single key server compromise provides complete access to all encrypted memories. Multi-server config provides no threshold benefit.
Remediation: Increase threshold to at least 2. Make configurable via environment variable.

Title: [MED-11] owner Address Not Validated in Walrus Upload
Description: /walrus/upload accepts owner address with no format validation. No auth means any caller can specify any Sui address as blob recipient at server wallet expense.
Remediation: Validate owner matches Sui address format. Auth on sidecar (HIGH-1) is primary fix.

Title: [MED-12] 50 MB JSON Body Limit on All Sidecar Endpoints
Description: Express JSON parser configured with 50MB limit globally. Multiple concurrent large payloads can exhaust Node.js heap, making sidecar unresponsive.
Remediation: Reduce global limit to 5-10MB. Consider per-endpoint limits.

Title: [MED-13] No Array Size Limit on /seal/decrypt-batch
Description: items array has no max size. Each element triggers CPU-intensive EncryptedObject.parse and SEAL key server calls. Thousands of entries can block Node.js event loop.
Remediation: Enforce maximum items limit (50-100) at handler entry point.

Title: [MED-14] Broad Semver Ranges on Security-Critical Dependencies
Description: All sidecar dependencies including @mysten/seal and @mysten/sui use caret semver ranges. Unexpected releases can alter encryption behavior.
Remediation: Pin exact versions for cryptographic dependencies. Commit lockfile.

Title: [MED-15] Unvalidated sui_address in add_delegate_key
Description: sui_address accepted as caller-supplied input with no on-chain validation it derives from public_key. Duplicate check enforces public_key uniqueness only, not address uniqueness.
Remediation: Derive sui_address on-chain from public_key, or require delegate co-signature. Add address uniqueness check.

Title: [MED-16] Delegate Path Skips Key ID Validation in seal_approve
Description: Owner path requires has_suffix(id, bcs(owner)) binding decryption to specific owner data. Delegate path checks only is_delegate_address with no ID validation.
Remediation: Apply same has_suffix(id, owner_bytes) check to delegate authorization path.

Title: [MED-17] Private Key Accepted as Immutable JavaScript String
Description: SDK config types accept private keys as hex strings. JS strings are immutable and cannot be zeroed; key persists in V8 heap until GC.
Remediation: Accept Uint8Array as alternative input type. Provide destroy() method that zeroes buffer.

Title: [MED-18] Default Server URL Uses Plaintext HTTP
Description: SDK defaults to http://localhost:8000 with no enforcement or warning for non-localhost HTTP in production. Combined with CRIT-1, private key travels unencrypted.
Remediation: Emit console warning or throw when serverUrl is non-HTTPS and host is not localhost.

Title: [MED-19] Rate Limit Check-Then-Record TOCTOU Race
Description: Middleware checks all three rate limit windows before recording any entries. Concurrent requests can complete check phase simultaneously, bypassing limits in burst.
Remediation: Replace check-then-record with atomic Redis Lua script.

Title: [MED-20] Endpoint Weight Bypassable via Path Variation
Description: endpoint_weight performs exact string matches. Trailing slash or URL-encoded variant causes expensive endpoints to fall to default weight 1, bypassing 10x cost.
Remediation: Normalize paths before matching or attach cost weights as Axum route extensions.

Title: [MED-21] Storage Quota Check-Then-Write Race Condition
Description: check_storage_quota reads usage from PostgreSQL then caller proceeds independently. Concurrent requests pass same quota baseline before any record consumption.
Remediation: Use PostgreSQL advisory lock per owner or Redis reservation system.

Title: [MED-22] Container Runs as Root / Remote Script Execution in Dockerfile
Description: No USER directive — server and sidecar run as root. Dockerfile fetches and executes remote shell script via curl | bash with no hash verification.
Remediation: Add non-root appuser. Replace curl-pipe-bash with official Node.js base image.

Title: [LOW-1] Query string excluded from request signature
Description: Path signed without query params. No current routes affected but pattern is fragile for future endpoints.
Remediation: Use path_and_query() in signed message.

Title: [LOW-2] Config fallback account resolution timing side-channel
Description: Response timing may differ between key-not-found and account-not-exist in Strategy 3.
Remediation: Normalize response timing across fallback paths.

Title: [LOW-3] Stale delegate key cache not removed on revocation
Description: Revoked keys re-check on-chain every request but stale row persists in DB indefinitely.
Remediation: Add TTL-based eviction to delegate key cache.

Title: [LOW-4] Non-atomic rate limit record pipeline
Description: ZADD + EXPIRE not wrapped in .atomic(). Partial pipeline failure can leave keys with no TTL.
Remediation: Wrap ZADD + EXPIRE in .atomic() pipeline.

Title: [LOW-5] AuthInfo derives Debug leaking delegate key
Description: Any {:?} format of AuthInfo prints raw private key hex to logs.
Remediation: Implement manual Debug that redacts delegate_key field.

Title: [LOW-6] No text length cap on /api/remember
Description: Input bounded only by 1MB body limit. Oversized payloads waste OpenAI embedding quota.
Remediation: Add explicit text length validation.

Title: [LOW-7] Silent result drop in /api/recall
Description: Failed blob downloads silently omitted. Client cannot distinguish errors from empty results.
Remediation: Include error indicators in response for failed downloads.

Title: [LOW-8] Indirect prompt injection via stored memories in /api/ask
Description: User-stored memories injected into LLM context without delimiter. Low risk as it is own data only.
Remediation: Add clear delimiters between memory context and user query.

Title: [LOW-9] No timeout on LLM / embedding HTTP calls
Description: reqwest::Client has no configured timeout. Slow LLM responses block handler indefinitely.
Remediation: Configure timeouts on reqwest clients for LLM and embedding calls.

Title: [LOW-10] delete_by_blob_id not owner-scoped
Description: Deletes any matching blob_id regardless of owner. Collision risk negligible but exists.
Remediation: Add owner filter to delete_by_blob_id query.

Title: [LOW-11] Storage quota pre-check uses plaintext byte length
Description: Quota checked against raw text size. SEAL encryption overhead not accounted for.
Remediation: Account for SEAL encryption overhead in quota check.

Title: [LOW-12] Private key hex parsed without validation in sidecar
Description: parseInt(b, 16) returns NaN for invalid hex. Produces silent zero bytes in key material.
Remediation: Validate hex string before parsing. Reject invalid input.

Title: [LOW-13] SessionKey TTL set to 30 minutes
Description: Decrypt session keys remain valid 30 min. Single-request operation needs ≤2 min.
Remediation: Reduce ttlMin to 2-5 minutes.

Title: [LOW-14] Metadata/transfer failure after Walrus upload is silent
Description: Upload reports success even when blob object transfer to owner fails.
Remediation: Propagate transfer failure as error in upload response.

Title: [LOW-15] Sponsor digest interpolated directly into Enoki URL path
Description: No format validation before path interpolation.
Remediation: Validate digest format before URL interpolation.

Title: [LOW-16] No packageId format validation
Description: Malformed Move call targets cause opaque transaction errors.
Remediation: Validate packageId format at handler entry.

Title: [LOW-17] epochs parameter accepted without bounds
Description: Caller can request excessive storage epochs increasing SUI cost to server wallet.
Remediation: Cap epochs at a reasonable maximum (e.g. 5).

Title: [LOW-18] Sensitive values in sidecar console logs
Description: Sender addresses, blob object IDs, and error details logged via console.log/error.
Remediation: Remove sensitive values from log output. Log only metadata.

Title: [LOW-19] Contract: deactivate_account not idempotent
Description: Re-deactivating already-deactivated account emits spurious AccountDeactivated event.
Remediation: Add guard: assert!(account.active == true) before deactivation.

Title: [LOW-20] Contract: deactivation prevents delegate key removal
Description: remove_delegate_key requires active == true. Owner cannot purge compromised key after deactivation.
Remediation: Allow remove_delegate_key on deactivated accounts.

Title: [LOW-21] Contract: no delegate key label length validation
Description: Labels are arbitrary-length strings. Only Sui 128KB transaction limit applies.
Remediation: Add maximum label length validation.

Title: [LOW-22] SDK: default server URL is plaintext HTTP
Description: Default http://localhost:8000 used without HTTPS enforcement in non-localhost configs.
Remediation: Warn or throw for non-HTTPS non-localhost URLs.

Title: [LOW-23] SDK: x-account-id header not part of signed message
Description: Intermediary can swap account hint without breaking signature.
Remediation: Include x-account-id in signed message payload.

Title: [LOW-24] SDK: SEAL encryption ID is owner-scoped not namespace-scoped
Description: Delegates authorized for one namespace can request SEAL decryption across all namespaces.
Remediation: Include namespace in SEAL encryption identity.

Title: [LOW-25] SDK: hexToBytes silently accepts invalid hex
Description: Non-hex characters produce NaN → 0 bytes. Corrupted keys fail silently.
Remediation: Validate hex input. Throw on invalid characters.

Title: [LOW-26] SDK: server error messages propagated verbatim to callers
Description: Internal server details (stack traces, RPC URLs) surface in client-thrown exceptions.
Remediation: Sanitize error messages before throwing to callers.

Title: [LOW-27] Docker Compose: PostgreSQL and Redis exposed to all interfaces
Description: Both services bind 0.0.0.0. Dev credentials hardcoded in committed file.
Remediation: Bind to 127.0.0.1. Use secrets management. Add dev-only comment.

Title: [LOW-28] Docker: container base images not pinned by digest
Description: Mutable tags rust:1.85-bookworm and debian:bookworm-slim allow silent tag mutation.
Remediation: Pin base images by digest.

Title: [LOW-29] Docker: no resource limits on containers
Description: Runaway queries can consume all host memory and CPU.
Remediation: Add memory and CPU limits to docker-compose services.

Title: [LOW-30] App: partial private key exposed in Dashboard code snippets
Description: First and last 8 hex chars of delegate key rendered in copyable SDK examples.
Remediation: Redact key entirely or use placeholder in code snippets.

Title: [LOW-31] App: key label input accepts arbitrary content
Description: No length or character restriction. Stored on-chain. Potential XSS in non-React consumers.
Remediation: Add length and character validation to label input.

Title: [LOW-32] App: no session expiry or key-wipe on inactivity
Description: clearDelegateKeys defined but never invoked on logout or tab close.
Remediation: Invoke clearDelegateKeys on logout and tab close events.

Title: [LOW-33] Chatbot: cookie set without Secure / SameSite attributes
Description: chat-model cookie sent over HTTP in non-HTTPS deployments.
Remediation: Set Secure and SameSite attributes on cookie.

Title: [LOW-34] Chatbot: file upload endpoint has no rate limit
Description: Authenticated users can upload indefinitely exhausting blob storage budget.
Remediation: Add per-user rate limiting to upload endpoint.

Title: [INFO-1] Stale cache entries not evicted on delegate key revocation
Description: Performance waste. No security impact due to mandatory on-chain re-verification.
Remediation: Add TTL-based eviction.

Title: [INFO-2] Integer overflow in timestamp subtraction (debug builds)
Description: i64::MIN timestamp causes panic in debug mode. Wraps harmlessly in release.
Remediation: Use checked_sub or saturating_sub for timestamp arithmetic.

Title: [INFO-3] DelegateKeyRemoved event does not include sui_address
Description: Indexer must correlate with DelegateKeyAdded to determine which address lost access.
Remediation: Include sui_address in DelegateKeyRemoved event.

Title: [INFO-4] Account registry entries are permanent (no deletion)
Description: Intentional design. Registry grows monotonically with no cleanup path.
Remediation: Intentional. Document in architecture docs.

Title: [INFO-5] Missing contract test coverage for five edge cases
Description: No tests for: non-owner key removal, non-owner reactivation, max-key boundary, seal_approve wrong ID, duplicate sui_address.
Remediation: Add test cases for listed edge cases.

Title: [INFO-6] Sidecar /health endpoint discloses process uptime
Description: process.uptime() returned in health response. Minor operational disclosure.
Remediation: Remove uptime from health response or restrict to internal callers.

Title: [INFO-7] SDK health check is unsigned
Description: A MitM can return fake healthy status. No authenticated endpoint validation.
Remediation: Document limitation. Consider signed health check.
